title: Visit Luke in Parliament with your school - Luke Pollard MP
h1: Visit Luke in Parliament with your school
meta_description: Labour & Co-op Member of Parliament for Plymouth Sutton & Devonport
text_excerpt: Visit Luke in Parliament with your school - Luke Pollard MP Skip to main content Luke Pollard MP Labour & Co-op Member of Parliament for Plymouth Sutton & Devonport Join Get Involved Donate About Luke News Keyham’s Law £20 million extra for Devonport, Mount Wise and Morice Town Armada Way trees First Homes Not Second Homes Plymouth Buildings at Risk Make Devil’s Point a Bathing Water Electric Blanket Fundraiser smashes past the £6,000 mark National Marine Park The Plymouth White Papers Luke in Parliament General Election Results for 2024, 2019 and 2017 Get in touch Contact #MeetLuke Visit Luke in Parliament with your school Privacy Policy Social Media Policy Support Support with damp and mould issues Cost of living - advice Cost of living - support in Plymouth Sign up to Luke's Newsletter Join #Team Luke Ebrington Street survey Pride in Place: Morice Town, Mount Wise and Devonport More Search Search the website Search Menu Visit Luke in Parliament with your school Luke with students from Devonport High School for Girls Come visit Luke in the Houses of Parliament with your school and learn what Members do as your representatives. The Parliamentary Education Centre offers visits during term time, Monday to Friday from 9am to 3pm . You can choose between two types of visits: A film and a tour A tour and a workshop Bookings can be made online a term in advance here . The education centre also takes short notice bookings by phone on 020 7219 4496 . Their advisors will be able to talk you through the programmes available and recommend the most suitable option for you and your class. State-funded schools from Plymouth are entitled to claim back up to 85% for travel expenses up to a maximum claim of £2400 (per school per financial year). You can also claim if your child is home educated. More information about the tour, films and workshops is listed on the Parliamentary Education Centre’s web page . About UK Parliament Education They aim to inform, engage and empower young people to understand and get involved in the work of their UK Parliament. Their dedicated Education Centre provides young people with the opportunity to visit and learn about their democracy through an engaging and diverse series of workshops. They also run an outreach programme in schools across the UK, provide teacher training and offer a range of curriculum based resources to support teaching about Parliament and democracy. The UK Parliament’s schools programme is informed by a development framework . This framework places our key messages in the appropriate curricular context, maximising Parliament’s educational value to teachers and learners. Stay in touch with Parliament Education Email the bookings team Email the teacher training team Subscribe to their monthly e-newsletter Follow @UKParlEducation on Twitter Discover more resources for your school here Luke Pollard MP Contact details Luke Pollard, 13 Frankfort Gate, Plymouth, PL1 1QA Luke Pollard, 13 Frankfort Gate, Plymouth, PL1 1QA '> View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Printed and Promoted by Chris Cuddihee, on behalf of Luke Pollard, Lockyer Hall, Alfred Street, Plymouth PL1 2RP.
